guidance.whenToUse: Use Tabs to organize a large amount of related content into a smaller, more manageable space, allowing users to switch between different views without navigating to a new page.
guidance.accessibility: Tabs are inherently accessible when built correctly, leveraging ARIA `role="tablist"`, `role="tab"`, and `role="tabpanel"` attributes. Ensure keyboard navigation (arrow keys for tab selection, Tab key to move into tab panel content) is fully supported. Each tab should have a unique ID and `aria-controls` pointing to its corresponding tab panel.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use Tabs for unrelated content or when content needs to be visible simultaneously. Consider Accordion for less critical content or multi-step forms. Avoid using tabs for navigation that leads to entirely different pages; use a NavLink or Menu instead.

response.description: A component for organizing and navigating between related content sections within a confined space.
response.semanticDescription: The Tabs component provides an effective way to structure and present content in a limited screen area. It allows users to group related information into distinct sections, each accessible via a clickable tab header. This component is ideal for dashboards, profile settings, product details, or any interface where multiple views of information need to be available without full page reloads. Key features include active tab indication, keyboard navigation support, and semantic ARIA attributes for accessibility. It improves user experience by reducing clutter and streamlining content organization.

minimal.description: A minimal example of the Tabs component.
